parsed_artifacts.content:
{"files":[{"path":"caa_timer/yaml/caa_timer.ssot.yaml","kind":"ssot","content":"top_module:
  name: caa_timer
  file: rtl/caa_timer.sv
  version: "1.0"
  type: peripheral
  description: APB3 programmable 8-bit timer with compare-triggered level interrupt
  reference_spec: caa_timer/req/caa_timer_requirements.md
  target:
    technology: generic
    clock_freq_mhz: 200
    area_um2: null
    power_mw: null

rtl_contract:
  implementation_owner: common_ai_agent.rtl-gen
  authoring_policy: RTL must be authored by rtl-gen with real LLM calls; deterministic fixed-template fallback is disallowed.
  top_module_name_lock: caa_timer
  top_module_submodule_name_collision_forbidden: true
  notes:
    - Top module is caa_timer and sub_modules must not contain another entry named exactly caa_timer.

sub_modules:
  - name: caa_timer_apb
    file: rtl/caa_timer_apb.sv
    ownership: manifest
    ssot_gen: true
    implements:
      - io_list.interfaces.apb_slave
      - registers.register_list
      - error_handling.error_sources.ILLEGAL_ADDR
    source_sections:
      - io_list
      - registers
      - error_handling
    register_refs:
      - registers.register_list
    description: APB access decode, legal/illegal response generation, register read/write muxing
    connections:
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: paddr
        signal: paddr
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: psel
        signal: psel
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: penable
        signal: penable
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: pwrite
        signal: pwrite
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: pwdata
        signal: pwdata
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: prdata
        signal: prdata
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: pready
        signal: pready
      - module: caa_timer_apb
        port: pslverr
        signal: pslverr
  - name: caa_timer_core
    file: rtl/caa_timer_core.sv
    ownership: manifest
    ssot_gen: false
    implements:
      - function_model.transactions.TR_CFG_WRITE
      - function_model.transactions.TR_COUNT_TICK
      - function_model.transactions.TR_COMPARE_HIT
      - function_model.transactions.TR_IRQ_CLEAR
      - function_model.transactions.TR_ILLEGAL_ACCESS
      - cycle_model.pipeline
      - interrupts.sources
      - fsm.timer_control
    source_sections:
      - function_model
      - cycle_model
      - interrupts
      - fsm
      - features
      - dataflow
      - decomposition
    function_model_refs:
      - function_model.transactions.TR_CFG_WRITE
      - function_model.transactions.TR_COUNT_TICK
      - function_model.transactions.TR_COMPARE_HIT
      - function_model.transactions.TR_IRQ_CLEAR
      - function_model.transactions.TR_ILLEGAL_ACCESS
      - function_model.state_variables
    cycle_model_refs:
      - cycle_model.pipeline
      - cycle_model.handshake_rules
      - cycle_model.ordering
    fsm_refs:
      - fsm.timer_control
    dataflow_refs:
      - dataflow.counter_path
      - dataflow.irq_path
    description: Counter increment, compare match detection, irq pending latch/set/clear
  - name: caa_timer_top
    file: rtl/caa_timer_top.sv
    ownership: manifest
    ssot_gen: true
    wiring_only: true
    implements:
      - integration.connections
      - io_list.interfaces
      - io_list.clock_domains
      - io_list.resets
    source_sections:
      - io_list
      - integration
    description: Optional wiring wrapper reserved by engineering rule

decomposition:
  units:
    - id: apb_frontend
      kind: control
      source_refs:
        - io_list.interfaces.apb_slave
        - registers.register_list
        - error_handling.error_sources
      rtl_candidates:
        - caa_timer_apb
      verification_impact:
        - test_requirements.scenarios
    - id: timer_engine
      kind: datapath/control
      source_refs:
        - function_model.transactions
        - cycle_model.pipeline
        - interrupts.sources
      rtl_candidates:
        - caa_timer_core
      verification_impact:
        - test_requirements.coverage_goals.function
        - test_requirements.coverage_goals.cycle

parameters:
  - name: ADDR_WIDTH
    default: 8
    type: int
    description: APB address width
    drives: [rtl/caa_timer.sv, rtl/caa_timer_apb.sv]
  - name: DATA_WIDTH
    default: 32
    type: int
    description: APB data width
    drives: [rtl/caa_timer.sv, rtl/caa_timer_apb.sv]
  - name: COUNTER_WIDTH
    default: 8
    type: int
    description: Timer counter/compare width
    drives: [rtl/caa_timer_core.sv]

io_list:
  clock_domains:
    - name: pclk
      frequency_mhz: 200
      description: APB peripheral clock
      ports:
        - { name: pclk, width: 1, direction: input, description: peripheral clock }
  resets:
    - name: presetn
      polarity: active_low
      sync_async: async_assert_sync_deassert
      description: active-low asynchronous assert, synchronous deassert reset
      ports:
        - { name: presetn, width: 1, direction: input, description: active-low reset }
  interfaces:
    - name: apb_slave
      type: APB3
      role: slave
      description: APB register interface
      clock_domain: pclk
      reset_domain: presetn
      ports:
        - { name: paddr, width: 8, direction: input, description: byte address }
        - { name: psel, width: 1, direction: input, description: peripheral select }
        - { name: penable, width: 1, direction: input, description: access phase indicator }
        - { name: pwrite, width: 1, direction: input, description: write indicator }
        - { name: pwdata, width: 32, direction: input, description: write data }
        - { name: prdata, width: 32, direction: output, description: read data }
        - { name: pready, width: 1, direction: output, description: ready response }
        - { name: pslverr, width: 1, direction: output, description: error response }
    - name: irq_out
      type: custom
      role: output
      description: timer interrupt output
      clock_domain: pclk
      reset_domain: presetn
      ports:
        - { name: irq, width: 1, direction: output, description: interrupt pending level }

features:
  - name: enable_controlled_counter
    trigger: CONTROL.enable set to 1
    datapath: counter increments on each rising pclk while enabled
    control: gated by enable and reset
    output: VALUE[7:0] reports current counter
  - name: compare_interrupt_latch
    trigger: enabled counter equals COMPARE[7:0]
    datapath: compare match sets irq_pending latch
    control: irq_pending remains set until IRQ_STATUS.pending W1C clear
    output: irq mirrors irq_pending as active-high level
  - name: deterministic_apb_response
    trigger: APB access phase psel && penable
    datapath: decode 0x00 CONTROL, 0x04 COMPARE, 0x08 VALUE, 0x0C IRQ_STATUS
    control: one access-phase completion for legal and illegal addresses
    output: legal pslverr=0, illegal pslverr=1 and prdata=0

dataflow:
  register_write_path:
    source: apb_slave.pwdata
    destination: CONTROL.enable and COMPARE.compare and IRQ_STATUS.pending clear
    sequence: APB decode -> legal write acceptance -> sequential state update
  counter_path:
    source: enable state
    destination: VALUE.current_count
    sequence: if enable == 1 then counter_next = (counter + 1) & 0xFF else hold
  irq_path:
    source: compare_match and IRQ_STATUS W1C clear
    destination: irq_pending and irq output
    sequence: set on compare match, clear only on legal write of 1 to IRQ_STATUS.pending

function_model:
  purpose: Executable architectural behavior for timer and APB-visible effects.
  state_variables:
    - { name: enable, source: registers.register_list.CONTROL.fields.enable, reset: 0, width: 1, description: counter enable }
    - { name: compare, source: registers.register_list.COMPARE.fields.compare, reset: 3, width: 8, description: compare threshold }
    - { name: counter, source: registers.register_list.VALUE.fields.value, reset: 0, width: 8, description: 8-bit counter state }
    - { name: irq_pending, source: registers.register_list.IRQ_STATUS.fields.pending, reset: 0, width: 1, description: latched pending interrupt }
  transactions:
    - id: TR_CFG_WRITE
      name: write_control_or_compare
      preconditions:
        - apb_valid_write and ((addr == 0) or (addr == 4))
      inputs: [addr, pwdata]
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pready }
        - { name: pslverr_out, expr: 0, width: 1, port: pslverr }
      state_updates:
        - { name: enable_next, expr: ((pwdata & 1) if (addr == 0) else enable), width: 1 }
        - { name: compare_next, expr: ((pwdata & 0xFF) if (addr == 4) else compare), width: 8 }
      side_effects:
        - CONTROL.enable updated on addr 0x00 writes
        - COMPARE.compare updated on addr 0x04 writes
    - id: TR_COUNT_TICK
      name: increment_counter_when_enabled
      preconditions:
        - tick_edge == 1
      inputs: [enable, counter]
      output_rules:
        - { name: value_read_data, expr: counter, width: 8, port: VALUE.value }
      state_updates:
        - { name: counter_next, expr: (((counter + 1) & 0xFF) if (enable == 1) else counter), width: 8 }
      side_effects:
        - counter increments once per clock while enable is 1
    - id: TR_COMPARE_HIT
      name: set_irq_on_compare_match
      preconditions:
        - tick_edge == 1
        - enable == 1
        - counter == compare
      inputs: [counter, compare, enable]
      output_rules:
        - { name: irq_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: irq }
      state_updates:
        - { name: irq_pending_next_set, expr: 1, width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - irq_pending is set when counter equals compare while enabled
    - id: TR_IRQ_CLEAR
      name: clear_irq_status_w1c
      preconditions:
        - wr_irq_status and ((pwdata & 1) == 1)
      inputs: [pwdata, irq_pending]
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pready }
        - { name: pslverr_out, expr: 0, width: 1, port: pslverr }
      state_updates:
        - { name: irq_pending_next_clear, expr: 0, width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - writing 1 to IRQ_STATUS.pending clears irq_pending
    - id: TR_ILLEGAL_ACCESS
      name: illegal_address_response
      preconditions:
        - apb_access and (not legal_addr)
      inputs: [addr]
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pready }
        - { name: pslverr_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pslverr }
        - { name: prdata_out, expr: 0, width: 32, port: prdata }
      state_updates: []
      side_effects:
        - no architectural state change
  invariants:
    - irq_pending in [0, 1]
    - (enable == 0) implies (counter_hold_if_no_tick == counter)
    - illegal APB access never modifies enable, compare, counter, or irq_pending
    - writes to VALUE address 0x08 are ignored and do not assert pslverr
  derived_signals:
    - { name: apb_access, expr: psel and penable, width: 1, description: APB access phase }
    - { name: apb_valid_write, expr: psel and penable and pwrite, width: 1, description: APB write access }
    - { name: apb_valid_read, expr: psel and penable and (not pwrite), width: 1, description: APB read access }
    - { name: addr, expr: paddr, width: 8, description: APB byte address }
    - { name: legal_addr, expr: (addr == 0) or (addr == 4) or (addr == 8) or (addr == 12), width: 1, description: legal address decode }
    - { name: wr_irq_status, expr: apb_valid_write and (addr == 12), width: 1, description: IRQ_STATUS write decode }
    - { name: rd_control, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 0), width: 1, description: CONTROL read decode }
    - { name: rd_compare, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 4), width: 1, description: COMPARE read decode }
    - { name: rd_value, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 8), width: 1, description: VALUE read decode }
    - { name: rd_irq_status, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 12), width: 1, description: IRQ_STATUS read decode }
    - { name: read_mux, expr: ((enable & 1) if (addr == 0) else ((compare & 0xFF) if (addr == 4) else ((counter & 0xFF) if (addr == 8) else ((irq_pending & 1) if (addr == 12) else 0)))), width: 32, description: APB read data mux }

cycle_model:
  purpose: Cycle/handshake timing contract for APB response and timer state updates.
  executable: pymtl3
  backend_policy: Use PyMTL3 shell for cycle scheduling; function_model remains behavior oracle.
  clock: pclk
  reset:
    assertion: presetn low asynchronously clears enable counter irq_pending and sets compare to 3
    deassertion: state is usable on first rising pclk edge after synchronized deassertion
  latency:
    apb_legal_access: { min_cycles: 1, max_cycles: 1, description: one access phase with pready 1 }
    apb_illegal_access: { min_cycles: 1, max_cycles: 1, description: one access phase with pslverr 1 }
    irq_set_after_match: { min_cycles: 0, max_cycles: 1, description: irq_pending set on compare-match edge }
    irq_clear_w1c: { min_cycles: 0, max_cycles: 1, description: irq_pending clear on accepted W1C edge }
  handshake_rules:
    - { signal: pready, rule: (psel and penable) implies (pready == 1) }
    - { signal: pslverr, rule: pslverr == 1 iff (psel and penable and (not ((paddr == 0) or (paddr == 4) or (paddr == 8) or (paddr == 12)))) }
    - { signal: prdata, rule: if (psel and penable and (not pwrite) and (not ((paddr == 0) or (paddr == 4) or (paddr == 8) or (paddr == 12)))) then (prdata == 0) }
    - { signal: irq, rule: irq == irq_pending }
  pipeline:
    - { stage: S0_COUNT, cycle: t, action: if enable then compute counter_next }
    - { stage: S1_APB_DECODE, cycle: t, action: decode address and legality during access phase }
    - { stage: S2_APB_RESPOND, cycle: t, action: drive pready prdata pslverr and apply legal write effects }
    - { stage: S3_IRQ_UPDATE, cycle: t, action: set irq_pending on compare hit or clear on IRQ_STATUS W1C }
  ordering:
    - compare-hit set of irq_pending occurs on same rising edge as counter evaluation
    - IRQ_STATUS W1C clear applies only for legal address 0x0C write with pwdata bit0 1
    - illegal access response is side-effect-free and does not alter timer state
  backpressure:
    - APB slave does not stall in access phase because pready is always 1 when selected
  observability:
    - every function_model transaction maps to at least one cycle_model pipeline stage and one scenario

clock_reset_domains:
  domains:
    - { name: pclk, frequency_mhz: 200, description: timer/APB clock }
  reset_scheme:
    signal: presetn
    polarity: active_low
    type: async_assert_sync_deassert

cdc_requirements:
  crossings: []
  synchronizers: []
  note: Single clock domain; no CDC.

rdc_requirements:
  crossings: []
  synchronizers: []
  note: Single reset domain; no RDC.

registers:
  config:
    register_width: 32
    addr_width: 8
    byte_addressable: true
  register_list:
    - name: CONTROL
      offset: 0x00
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000000
      description: Control register
      fields:
        - { name: enable, bits: [0, 0], access: rw, reset: 0, description: enable counting }
    - name: COMPARE
      offset: 0x04
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000003
      description: Compare threshold register
      fields:
        - { name: compare, bits: [7, 0], access: rw, reset: 3, description: compare value for irq set }
    - name: VALUE
      offset: 0x08
      width: 32
      access: ro
      reset: 0x00000000
      description: Current counter value
      write_behavior: ignored
      fields:
        - { name: value, bits: [7, 0], access: ro, reset: 0, description: live 8-bit timer count }
    - name: IRQ_STATUS
      offset: 0x0C
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000000
      description: Interrupt pending status
      fields:
        - name: pending
          bits: [0, 0]
          access: rw1c
          reset: 0
          description: pending interrupt flag
          write_effect: Writing 1 clears bit; writing 0 leaves it unchanged.

memory:
  instances:
    - { name: counter_ff, type: register, depth: 1, width: 8, read_ports: 1, write_ports: 1, latency: 0, description: counter storage }
    - { name: compare_ff, type: register, depth: 1, width: 8, read_ports: 1, write_ports: 1, latency: 0, description: compare storage }
  note: No SRAM/FIFO required.

interrupts:
  sources:
    - { name: TIMER_COMPARE, bit: 0, type: level, enable_reg: null, status_reg: IRQ_STATUS, clear: W1C, description: set when counter equals compare while enabled }
  output:
    signal: irq
    polarity: active_high
    type: level

fsm:
  timer_control:
    states: [DISABLED, RUNNING]
    transitions:
      - { from: DISABLED, to: RUNNING, condition: enable == 1 }
      - { from: RUNNING, to: DISABLED, condition: enable == 0 }
    note: two-state enable control; irq_pending is orthogonal latch state

timing:
  target_clocks:
    - { domain: pclk, target_freq_mhz: 200, duty_cycle_pct: 50 }
  latency_budget:
    apb_access_cycles: 1
    irq_assert_cycles_from_match: 1
    irq_clear_cycles_from_w1c: 1
  throughput:
    counter_increment_per_cycle: 1
  sta_expectations:
    - setup and hold clean at target clock for all register-to-register paths

power:
  domains:
    - name: PD_MAIN
      supply: VDD
      elements: [caa_timer_apb, caa_timer_core]
      always_on: true
  power_states:
    - name: ON
      condition: presetn in [0, 1]
      behavior: functional except while reset asserted
    - name: RESET
      condition: presetn == 0
      behavior: enable counter irq_pending cleared and compare reset to 3
  clock_gating:
    supported: false
    rationale: small deterministic always-clocked timer
  upf_required: false

security:
  classification: non_secure_peripheral
  assets:
    - timer configuration integrity
    - interrupt signaling correctness
    - APB register access correctness
  threat_model:
    - invalid APB address probes must not corrupt state
    - writes to read-only VALUE must not alter state
  assumptions:
    - APB master trust and privilege enforcement are external integration responsibilities
  privilege_model: Integrator-owned bus/firewall privilege control.

error_handling:
  error_sources:
    - id: ILLEGAL_ADDR
      condition: ap

raw_response:

    power_mw: null

rtl_contract:

  top_module_name_lock: caa_timer

parameters:
  - name: ADDR_WIDTH
    default: 8
    type: int
    description: APB address width
    drives: [rtl/caa_timer.sv, rtl/caa_timer_apb.sv]
  - name: DATA_WIDTH
    default: 32
    type: int
    description: APB data width
    drives: [rtl/caa_timer.sv, rtl/caa_timer_apb.sv]
  - name: COUNTER_WIDTH
    default: 8
    type: int
    description: Timer counter/compare width
    drives: [rtl/caa_timer_core.sv]

io_list:
  clock_domains:
    - name: pclk
      frequency_mhz: 200
      description: APB peripheral clock
      ports:
        - { name: pclk, width: 1, direction: input, description: peripheral clock }
  resets:
    - name: presetn
      polarity: active_low
      sync_async: async_assert_sync_deassert
      description: active-low asynchronous assert, synchronous deassert reset
      ports:
        - { name: presetn, width: 1, direction: input, description: active-low reset }
  interfaces:
    - name: apb_slave
      type: APB3
      role: slave
      description: APB register interface
      clock_domain: pclk
      reset_domain: presetn
      ports:
        - { name: paddr, width: 8, direction: input, description: byte address }
        - { name: psel, width: 1, direction: input, description: peripheral select }
        - { name: penable, width: 1, direction: input, description: access phase indicator }
        - { name: pwrite, width: 1, direction: input, description: write indicator }
        - { name: pwdata, width: 32, direction: input, description: write data }
        - { name: prdata, width: 32, direction: output, description: read data }
        - { name: pready, width: 1, direction: output, description: ready response }
        - { name: pslverr, width: 1, direction: output, description: error response }
    - name: irq_out
      type: custom
      role: output
      description: timer interrupt output
      clock_domain: pclk
      reset_domain: presetn
      ports:
        - { name: irq, width: 1, direction: output, description: interrupt pending level }

features:
  - name: enable_controlled_counter
    trigger: CONTROL.enable set to 1
    datapath: counter increments on each rising pclk while enabled
    control: gated by enable and reset
    output: VALUE[7:0] reports current counter
  - name: compare_interrupt_latch
    trigger: enabled counter equals COMPARE[7:0]
    datapath: compare match sets irq_pending latch
    control: irq_pending remains set until IRQ_STATUS.pending W1C clear
    output: irq mirrors irq_pending as active-high level
  - name: deterministic_apb_response
    trigger: APB access phase psel && penable
    datapath: decode 0x00 CONTROL, 0x04 COMPARE, 0x08 VALUE, 0x0C IRQ_STATUS
    control: one access-phase completion for legal and illegal addresses
    output: legal pslverr=0, illegal pslverr=1 and prdata=0

dataflow:
  register_write_path:
    source: apb_slave.pwdata
    destination: CONTROL.enable and COMPARE.compare and IRQ_STATUS.pending clear
    sequence: APB decode -> legal write acceptance -> sequential state update
  counter_path:
    source: enable state
    destination: VALUE.current_count
    sequence: if enable == 1 then counter_next = (counter + 1) & 0xFF else hold
  irq_path:
    source: compare_match and IRQ_STATUS W1C clear
    destination: irq_pending and irq output
    sequence: set on compare match, clear only on legal write of 1 to IRQ_STATUS.pending

function_model:
  purpose: Executable architectural behavior for timer and APB-visible effects.
  state_variables:
    - { name: enable, source: registers.register_list.CONTROL.fields.enable, reset: 0, width: 1, description: counter enable }
    - { name: compare, source: registers.register_list.COMPARE.fields.compare, reset: 3, width: 8, description: compare threshold }
    - { name: counter, source: registers.register_list.VALUE.fields.value, reset: 0, width: 8, description: 8-bit counter state }
    - { name: irq_pending, source: registers.register_list.IRQ_STATUS.fields.pending, reset: 0, width: 1, description: latched pending interrupt }
  transactions:
    - id: TR_CFG_WRITE
      name: write_control_or_compare
      preconditions:
        - apb_valid_write and ((addr == 0) or (addr == 4))
      inputs: [addr, pwdata]
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pready }
        - { name: pslverr_out, expr: 0, width: 1, port: pslverr }
      state_updates:
        - { name: enable_next, expr: ((pwdata & 1) if (addr == 0) else enable), width: 1 }
        - { name: compare_next, expr: ((pwdata & 0xFF) if (addr == 4) else compare), width: 8 }
      side_effects:
        - CONTROL.enable updated on addr 0x00 writes
        - COMPARE.compare updated on addr 0x04 writes
    - id: TR_COUNT_TICK
      name: increment_counter_when_enabled
      preconditions:
        - tick_edge == 1
      inputs: [enable, counter]
      output_rules:
        - { name: value_read_data, expr: counter, width: 8, port: VALUE.value }
      state_updates:
        - { name: counter_next, expr: (((counter + 1) & 0xFF) if (enable == 1) else counter), width: 8 }
      side_effects:
        - counter increments once per clock while enable is 1
    - id: TR_COMPARE_HIT
      name: set_irq_on_compare_match
      preconditions:
        - tick_edge == 1
        - enable == 1
        - counter == compare
      inputs: [counter, compare, enable]
      output_rules:
        - { name: irq_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: irq }
      state_updates:
        - { name: irq_pending_next_set, expr: 1, width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - irq_pending is set when counter equals compare while enabled
    - id: TR_IRQ_CLEAR
      name: clear_irq_status_w1c
      preconditions:
        - wr_irq_status and ((pwdata & 1) == 1)
      inputs: [pwdata, irq_pending]
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pready }
        - { name: pslverr_out, expr: 0, width: 1, port: pslverr }
      state_updates:
        - { name: irq_pending_next_clear, expr: 0, width: 1 }
      side_effects:
        - writing 1 to IRQ_STATUS.pending clears irq_pending
    - id: TR_ILLEGAL_ACCESS
      name: illegal_address_response
      preconditions:
        - apb_access and (not legal_addr)
      inputs: [addr]
      output_rules:
        - { name: pready_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pready }
        - { name: pslverr_out, expr: 1, width: 1, port: pslverr }
        - { name: prdata_out, expr: 0, width: 32, port: prdata }
      state_updates: []
      side_effects:
        - no architectural state change
  invariants:
    - irq_pending in [0, 1]
    - (enable == 0) implies (counter_hold_if_no_tick == counter)
    - illegal APB access never modifies enable, compare, counter, or irq_pending
    - writes to VALUE address 0x08 are ignored and do not assert pslverr
  derived_signals:
    - { name: apb_access, expr: psel and penable, width: 1, description: APB access phase }
    - { name: apb_valid_write, expr: psel and penable and pwrite, width: 1, description: APB write access }
    - { name: apb_valid_read, expr: psel and penable and (not pwrite), width: 1, description: APB read access }
    - { name: addr, expr: paddr, width: 8, description: APB byte address }
    - { name: legal_addr, expr: (addr == 0) or (addr == 4) or (addr == 8) or (addr == 12), width: 1, description: legal address decode }
    - { name: wr_irq_status, expr: apb_valid_write and (addr == 12), width: 1, description: IRQ_STATUS write decode }
    - { name: rd_control, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 0), width: 1, description: CONTROL read decode }
    - { name: rd_compare, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 4), width: 1, description: COMPARE read decode }
    - { name: rd_value, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 8), width: 1, description: VALUE read decode }
    - { name: rd_irq_status, expr: apb_valid_read and (addr == 12), width: 1, description: IRQ_STATUS read decode }
    - { name: read_mux, expr: ((enable & 1) if (addr == 0) else ((compare & 0xFF) if (addr == 4) else ((counter & 0xFF) if (addr == 8) else ((irq_pending & 1) if (addr == 12) else 0)))), width: 32, description: APB read data mux }

cycle_model:
  purpose: Cycle/handshake timing contract for APB response and timer state updates.
  executable: pymtl3
  backend_policy: Use PyMTL3 shell for cycle scheduling; function_model remains behavior oracle.
  clock: pclk
  reset:
    assertion: presetn low asynchronously clears enable counter irq_pending and sets compare to 3
    deassertion: state is usable on first rising pclk edge after synchronized deassertion
  latency:
    apb_legal_access: { min_cycles: 1, max_cycles: 1, description: one access phase with pready 1 }
    apb_illegal_access: { min_cycles: 1, max_cycles: 1, description: one access phase with pslverr 1 }
    irq_set_after_match: { min_cycles: 0, max_cycles: 1, description: irq_pending set on compare-match edge }
    irq_clear_w1c: { min_cycles: 0, max_cycles: 1, description: irq_pending clear on accepted W1C edge }
  handshake_rules:
    - { signal: pready, rule: (psel and penable) implies (pready == 1) }
    - { signal: pslverr, rule: pslverr == 1 iff (psel and penable and (not ((paddr == 0) or (paddr == 4) or (paddr == 8) or (paddr == 12)))) }
    - { signal: prdata, rule: if (psel and penable and (not pwrite) and (not ((paddr == 0) or (paddr == 4) or (paddr == 8) or (paddr == 12)))) then (prdata == 0) }
    - { signal: irq, rule: irq == irq_pending }
  pipeline:
    - { stage: S0_COUNT, cycle: t, action: if enable then compute counter_next }
    - { stage: S1_APB_DECODE, cycle: t, action: decode address and legality during access phase }
    - { stage: S2_APB_RESPOND, cycle: t, action: drive pready prdata pslverr and apply legal write effects }
    - { stage: S3_IRQ_UPDATE, cycle: t, action: set irq_pending on compare hit or clear on IRQ_STATUS W1C }
  ordering:
    - compare-hit set of irq_pending occurs on same rising edge as counter evaluation
    - IRQ_STATUS W1C clear applies only for legal address 0x0C write with pwdata bit0 1
    - illegal access response is side-effect-free and does not alter timer state
  backpressure:
    - APB slave does not stall in access phase because pready is always 1 when selected
  observability:
    - every function_model transaction maps to at least one cycle_model pipeline stage and one scenario

clock_reset_domains:
  domains:
    - { name: pclk, frequency_mhz: 200, description: timer/APB clock }
  reset_scheme:
    signal: presetn
    polarity: active_low
    type: async_assert_sync_deassert

cdc_requirements:
  crossings: []
  synchronizers: []
  note: Single clock domain; no CDC.

rdc_requirements:
  crossings: []
  synchronizers: []
  note: Single reset domain; no RDC.

registers:
  config:
    register_width: 32
    addr_width: 8
    byte_addressable: true
  register_list:
    - name: CONTROL
      offset: 0x00
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000000
      description: Control register
      fields:
        - { name: enable, bits: [0, 0], access: rw, reset: 0, description: enable counting }
    - name: COMPARE
      offset: 0x04
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000003
      description: Compare threshold register
      fields:
        - { name: compare, bits: [7, 0], access: rw, reset: 3, description: compare value for irq set }
    - name: VALUE
      offset: 0x08
      width: 32
      access: ro
      reset: 0x00000000
      description: Current counter value
      write_behavior: ignored
      fields:
        - { name: value, bits: [7, 0], access: ro, reset: 0, description: live 8-bit timer count }
    - name: IRQ_STATUS
      offset: 0x0C
      width: 32
      access: rw
      reset: 0x00000000
      description: Interrupt pending status
      fields:
        - name: pending
          bits: [0, 0]
          access: rw1c
          reset: 0
          description: pending interrupt flag
          write_effect: Writing 1 clears bit; writing 0 leaves it unchanged.

memory:
  instances:
    - { name: counter_ff, type: register, depth: 1, width: 8, read_ports: 1, write_ports: 1, latency: 0, description: counter storage }
    - { name: compare_ff, type: register, depth: 1, width: 8, read_ports: 1, write_ports: 1, latency: 0, description: compare storage }
  note: No SRAM/FIFO required.

interrupts:
  sources:
    - { name: TIMER_COMPARE, bit: 0, type: level, enable_reg: null, status_reg: IRQ_STATUS, clear: W1C, description: set when counter equals compare while enabled }
  output:
    signal: irq
    polarity: active_high
    type: level

fsm:
  timer_control:
    states: [DISABLED, RUNNING]
    transitions:
      - { from: DISABLED, to: RUNNING, condition: enable == 1 }
      - { from: RUNNING, to: DISABLED, condition: enable == 0 }
    note: two-state enable control; irq_pending is orthogonal latch state

timing:
  target_clocks:
    - { domain: pclk, target_freq_mhz: 200, duty_cycle_pct: 50 }
  latency_budget:
    apb_access_cycles: 1
    irq_assert_cycles_from_match: 1
    irq_clear_cycles_from_w1c: 1
  throughput:
    counter_increment_per_cycle: 1
  sta_expectations:
    - setup and hold clean at target clock for all register-to-register paths

power:
  domains:
    - name: PD_MAIN
      supply: VDD
      elements: [caa_timer_apb, caa_timer_core]
      always_on: true
  power_states:
    - name: ON
      condition: presetn in [0, 1]
      behavior: functional except while reset asserted
    - name: RESET
      condition: presetn == 0
      behavior: enable counter irq_pending cleared and compare reset to 3
  clock_gating:
    supported: false
    rationale: small deterministic always-clocked timer
  upf_required: false

security:
  classification: non_secure_peripheral
  assets:
    - timer configuration integrity
    - interrupt signaling correctness
    - APB register access correctness
  threat_model:
    - invalid APB address probes must not corrupt state
    - writes to read-only VALUE must not alter state
  assumptions:
    - APB master trust and privilege enforcement are external integration responsibilities
  privilege_model: Integrator-owned bus/firewall privilege control.

error_handling:
  error_sources:
    - id: ILLEGAL_ADDR
      condition: ap
